item.title: Thinking in Imperative, Object Oriented and Functional way
item.description: Complexity is the root cause of majority of software problems. Unreliable softwares are known to be notoriously complex. A complex system is hard to understand and reason about. The harder a system is to understand the more likely we are to introduce more complexity. Complex code base is not only harder to test but testing is that much less effective. Programming Paradigms Different programming styles tries to deal with the complexity in their own way.

item.title: A Detour Called Monads
item.description: When learning Haskell, all of us are faced with this monad monster. I too have spent many days and night fighting it - hoping that it will grant me all the wisdom if I survive long enough. I have come to believe that the community as whole has created this monstor. And many of us have taken this detour to master the art of monstor slaying instead of going on ahead - just to realise later that the detour was unnesessory.

item.title: Understanding Monads Using Function Composition
item.description: Today I am going to commit a crime - I am about to write one more blog relating to Monads! Why? Even though I could use monads for a while now, I never got around to understanding how they are composing functions and why couldn’t we use the (.) function to compose? I wanted to understand Monads from composition view point. Let’s Dig In Lets start with simple function composition
